report_name: Nightspire Ransomware Claims Attack on Cedar Crest College

threat_actors.name: Nightspire
threat_actors.description: A newer ransomware group that operates a dark web data leak site to conduct double-extortion attacks. The group added Cedar Crest College to its leak site on July 14, 2026, to pressure the victim into paying a ransom.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Nightspire ransomware group's primary impact is the encryption of victim files, a core component of their ransomware attacks, to disrupt operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: As part of its double-extortion model, the Nightspire group allegedly exfiltrated data from Cedar Crest College to its data leak site to coerce a ransom payment.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware like Nightspire commonly deletes Volume Shadow Copies using tools like vssadmin to prevent easy system recovery from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: Permission Groups Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely perform discovery of high-privilege groups, such as using the 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain' command, to identify key targets for lateral movement and privilege escalation.

malware.name: Nightspire
malware.description: A ransomware family that appends a custom extension, such as '.nightspire', to encrypted files. It is used in double-extortion campaigns, with victim data posted to a dedicated leak site.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin tool is commonly abused by ransomware actors to delete Volume Shadow Copies, hindering system recovery efforts.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: PowerShell is used by attackers to disable security products or download additional tools from the internet.

tools.name: Net
tools.description: The 'net' utility is used for network resource management, including discovering domain administrative groups with commands like 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain'.

identities.name: Cedar Crest College
identities.description: A private liberal arts college in Allentown, Pennsylvania, targeted by the Nightspire ransomware group and listed on their data leak site.

infrastructure.name: Nightspire Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site operated by the Nightspire ransomware group to publicly name victims and leak their stolen data as part of a double-extortion strategy.

indicators.name: Nightspire Ransomware File Extension
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Nightspire Ransomware File Extension
id: 2b1c3d4e-5f6a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects files being renamed with the .nightspire extension, which is indicative of the Nightspire ransomware encrypting files.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-nightspire-hits-cedar-crest-college/
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.nightspire'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the '.nightspire' extension, a key indicator of Nightspire ransomware activity.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-nightspire-hits-cedar-crest-college/
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware encryption.

indicators.name: Domain Admin Group Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Domain Admin Group Discovery via Net Command
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net group' to enumerate members of the 'Domain Admins' group, a common reconnaissance technique.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-16
references:
  - https://hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-nightspire-hits-cedar-crest-college/
tags:
  - attack.t1069.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'group'
      - 'Domain Admins'
      - '/domain'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects attempts to enumerate the 'Domain Admins' group using the 'net' command, a common discovery technique.
